name: Nano-Hz gravitational-wave background (NANOGrav 15 yr)
requirement: Any candidate framework must accommodate the Hellings-Downs-correlated stochastic gravitational-wave signal detected by NANOGrav, EPTA, PPTA, and CPTA (3-4 sigma in the 15-year NANOGrav data). The signal is consistent with a population of supermassive-black-hole binaries but also with cosmological GW backgrounds (cosmic strings, inflationary GW, first-order phase transitions, dark-photon dark matter).

regime.description: Nano-Hz gravitational-wave frequencies (~10^-9 - 10^-7 Hz); years to decades observation timescales of millisecond pulsars.

current_bound.units: characteristic strain at 1/yr; gamma is timing residual power-law index
current_bound.reference: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav), 'The NANOGrav 15 yr Data Set: Evidence for a Gravitational-Wave Background', ApJL 951, L8 (2023), arXiv:2306.16213; EPTA Collaboration, A&A 678, A50 (2023), arXiv:2306.16214

what_it_excludes: If interpreted as new physics, constrains cosmic-string tension G mu < ~10^-7, certain phase-transition energy scales near the QCD scale, and ultralight-dark-matter coupling to spacetime metric. The strain amplitude is at the high end of pre-2023 SMBH-binary expectations; cosmological-source interpretations remain open and actively debated.

parameterization.name: Stochastic GW background characterized by Omega_GW(f) (energy density per log frequency) and angular correlation Hellings-Downs (HD) signature.
parameterization.reference: Hellings and Downs, ApJL 265, L39 (1983); Maggiore, 'Gravitational Waves Vol. 2' (Oxford 2018).
parameterization.summary: PTA timing residuals exhibit a quadrupolar correlation between pulsars as a function of angular separation; spectrum strain h_c(f) ~ A_GWB (f / yr^-1)^(-2/3) for SMBH binary expectation.

procedural.predicted_observable: Cross-correlated timing residual power vs angular separation; characteristic strain at f = 1/yr.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Source-dependent. SMBH-binary interpretation: amplitude consistent with ~10^9 M_sun mergers. Cosmic-string tension G mu < ~10^-7 if attributed to strings.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts a stochastic GW background source (cosmic strings, primordial phase transition, ultralight DM), compute Omega_GW(f) at PTA frequencies.
Predict whether the signal has the HD spatial correlation (genuine GW) or different (e.g. ULDM monopole, scalar-tensor scalar-mode contribution).

primary_references.citation: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav), 'The NANOGrav 15 yr Data Set: Evidence for a Gravitational-Wave Background', ApJL 951, L8 (2023)
EPTA Collaboration, 'The second data release from the European Pulsar Timing Array III. Search for gravitational wave signals', A&A 678, A50 (2023)
Afzal et al. (NANOGrav), 'The NANOGrav 15 yr Data Set: Search for Signals from New Physics', ApJL 951, L11 (2023)
